mission.title: fluent-bit: Amazon S3: Mismatch when reading HTTP header from GCS
mission.description: Amazon S3: Mismatch when reading HTTP header from GCS. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current fluent-bit deployment
steps.description:
Verify your fluent-bit version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
helm list -n fluent-bit 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working fluent-bit installation.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
While configuring our FluentBit instance, I reached a situation when the Ama

steps.title: Apply the fix for Amazon S3: Mismatch when reading HTTP header from GCS
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:23:21] [debug] [upstream] KA connection #28 to storage.googleapis.com:443 is connected
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:23:21] [debug] [http_client] not using http_proxy for header
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:23:21] [debug] [aws_credentials] Requesting credentials from the env provider..
---
Here FluentBit blocks for 4 minutes...
---
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:27:21] [error] [http_client] broken connection to storage.googleapis.com:443 ?
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:27:21] [debug] [upstream] KA connection #28 to storage.googleapis.com:443 is now 
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Amazon S3: Mismatch when reading HTTP header from GCS" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the source issue for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/fluent/fluent-bit/issues/8790

fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:23:21] [debug] [upstream] KA connection #28 to storage.googleapis.com:443 is connected
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:23:21] [debug] [http_client] not using http_proxy for header
fluent-bit[1343]: [2024/05/03 03:23:21] [debug] [aws_credentials] Requesting credentials from the env provider..
---
Here FluentBit blocks for 4 minutes...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluent-bit installed or the issue environment reproducible.
